description: Name of assembler, should be taken from the author list defined in the metadata NB: Should probably be replaced by an ID
Method or decision fields used for identifying or assigning microchromosomes (Such as B Chromosomes), where relevant.
Tool or workflow used for microchromosome identification or assignment, for example MicroFinder. NB: Should be replaced by an identifier to a reference
Comparator species, assembly or reference used for assembly, where applicable.
Short note on extra-numerary-chromosome assignment, choices, or review decisions.
How chromosome names or numbers were assigned during curation.
Comparator assembly, species or reference source used when chromosomes were named by synteny.
Comparator species, assembly or reference used for chromosome assignment, where applicable.
Number of autosomes recorded by curation.

Curation chromosome assignment summary from the curation/JIRA record. data_note may parse jira_perc_assem from this text, but that value is not used for the published percentage of assembly assigned to chromosomes.
Observed or recorded sex of the specimen used for assembly, where curated.
Curated sex chromosome complement, for example XX, XY, ZZ, ZW or Z0.
Sex chromosome labels identified in the assembly and reported in the genome note, for example X, X and Y, W and Z, or Z.
Comparator species, assembly or reference used for sex chromosome assignment, where applicable.
Assembly type recorded in the curation/JIRA record, NB: I'm not really sure what this means - does it make sense to include this for assemblies outside of Tol?
NB: I'm also not sure what this means
Free-text curator note from the curation record, including chromosome assignment rationale or assembly caveats.
Evidence or data types used during curation, for example Hi-C contact maps and JBrowse.
Manual interventions in the assembly, normalised per Giga-base
Breaks performed across assembly
Joins performed across assembly
Manual removals of haplotypic duplications during curation; used with breaks and joins to derive the curation intervention sentence.
Manual inversions recorded in the curation/JIRA record. data_note pulls this field in.
Change in the length of the assembly, can be positive or negative. Recorded in base pairs.
Percentage change in total assembly length after curation; used in the derived curation intervention sentence.
Percentage change in scaffold count after curation; used in the derived curation intervention sentence.
Change in the scaffold N50 metric, recorded in base-pairs.
Percentage change in scaffold N50 after curation; used in the derived curation intervention sentence.
Short summary description of the interventions made during manual curation and their effect on scaffold count, scaffold N50 and assembly length.
Generalised statistics about the contaminats removed, do we need to include a file for specific data?
Number of scaffolds marked as contaminant and removed from the assembly
Percentage of scaffolds marked as contaminant and removed from the assembly
Total length of scaffolds removed, recorded in base-pairs
